articles.summary: 提出OmniRetrieval，统一检索异构知识源（文本、表格、知识图谱等），克服现有检索器仅支持单一源和固定查询语言的局限。

articles.title: YoCausal: How Far is Video Generation from World Model? A Causality Perspective
articles.excerpt: As video diffusion models (VDMs) advance toward world models, a key question arises: do they truly understand causality, or merely overfit to statistical temporal patterns? articles.summary: 提出YoCausal，从因果视角评估视频扩散模型是否真正理解因果而非过拟合统计模式，基于真实数据弥补模拟到现实的差距。

articles.title: Why Far Looks Up: Probing Spatial Representation in Vision-Language Models

We introduce a representation-level analysis framework that constructs minimal co
articles.summary: 提出表示级分析框架，探测视觉语言模型的空间表征是否具备结构化3D理解，揭示其可能依赖统计捷径而非真正空间推理。

articles.title: GenClaw: Code-Driven Agentic Image Generation

articles.summary: 提出GenClaw，代码驱动的自主图像生成框架，通过代码生成实现可控可解释的图像生成流程，摆脱对黑盒模型的依赖。

articles.title: EarlyTom: Early Token Compression Completes Fast Video Understanding

articles.summary: 提出EarlyTom，通过早期视觉token压缩加速视频理解，在保持性能的同时大幅降低token数量，解决Video-LLM部署效率瓶颈。

articles.title: UniSteer: Text-Guided Flow Matching in Activation Space for Versatile LLM Steering
articles.excerpt: Activation-based control steers large language models (LLMs) by intervening on their internal representations during inference, and has emerged as an effective paradigm for controlling behaviors such as persona and style. However, existing methods often rely on fixed steering directions or task-spec
articles.summary: 提出UniSteer，通过文本引导的激活空间流匹配实现LLM行为的灵活控制，克服固定方向或任务特定编辑的局限。

articles.title: LoMo: Local Modality Substitution for Deeper Vision-Language Fusion
articles.excerpt: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved substantial progress across a wide range of understanding and reasoning tasks, driven by large-scale image-text training aimed at multimodal fusion. Ideally, replacing a textual question with its rendered-image counterpart should leave model performance es
articles.summary: 提出LoMo，通过局部模态替换（将文本问题替换为渲染图像）测试视觉语言模型的多模态融合深度，揭示当前模型融合不充分。

articles.title: Skill0.5: Joint Skill Internalization and Utilization for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
articles.excerpt: Equipping large language models with explicit skills has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling autonomous agents to solve complex tasks. Agent skills can be inherently divided into general skills for broad cognitive transfer and task-specific skills for dynamic execution. However, existing sk
articles.summary: 提出Skill0.5，联合技能内化与利用框架，使LLM agent在强化学习中同时掌握通用认知技能和任务特定技能，提升分布外泛化能力。

articles.title: Xetrieval: Mechanistically Explaining Dense Retrieval
articles.excerpt: Explaining why dense retrievers assign high relevance scores remains challenging because retrieval decisions are made through opaque high-dimensional embeddings. Existing explanations often focus on surface signals, such as lexical matches, token alignments, or post-hoc textual rationales, and thus
articles.summary: 提出Xetrieval，从机制层面解释密集检索模型为何赋予高相关性分数，超越词汇匹配等表面信号，提供可解释的检索决策。

articles.title: Colored Noise Diffusion Sampling

Conventional stochastic differential equation (SDE) solvers fail to account f
articles.summary: 提出Colored Noise Diffusion采样方法，通过匹配扩散轨迹的谱偏差（低频先、高频后），改进传统SDE求解器，提升图像生成质量。

articles.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
articles.excerpt: We introduce CausaLab, a scalable environment for evaluating interactive causal discovery by LLM agents. Unlike prior evaluations, CausaLab evaluates both whether an agent can solve a problem using causal evidence and whether its answer is grounded in a faithful recovered causal mechanism. Each epis
articles.summary: 提出CausaLab，一个可扩展的交互式因果发现环境，评估LLM agent能否利用因果证据解决问题并忠实于恢复的因果机制。

articles.excerpt: The design space of agentic AI inference spans two extremes: frontier large language models (LLMs), typically hosted in the cloud and offering strong performance across a wide range of tasks at substantially high cost, and more cost-efficient small language models (SLMs), which are amenable to on-de
articles.summary: 探讨云端大模型与设备端小模型组成的混合多智能体系统设计，在性能与成本之间取得平衡，总结实践教训。

articles.excerpt: We address the task of generating physically accurate and visually faithful 4D Human-Object Interaction (HOI). articles.summary: 提出PhyGenHOI，物理感知的4D人-物交互生成，从静态3D人体和物体（3DGS）合成动态交互，确保物理准确性和视觉真实感。

articles.title: Towards Verifiable Multimodal Deep Research: A Multi-Agent Harness for Interleaved Report Generation
articles.excerpt: Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced autonomous agents from deep search, which retrieves concise factual answers, to deep research, which synthesizes scattered evidence into long-form reports. However, verifiable multimodal deep research remains challenging due to open-ended synthesis without
articles.summary: 提出一个多智能体框架，用于生成可验证的多模态深度研究报告，解决开放域合成缺乏可验证性的问题，结合检索与引用。

Small GUI agents that can be deployed directly on mobile devices are more attr
articles.summary: 提出UI-KOBE，知识导向的行为探索框架，利用图引导实现轻量级移动GUI agent，无需依赖大型视觉语言模型。

articles.title: RUBRIC-ARROW: Alternating Pointwise Rubric Reward Modeling for LLM Post-training in Non-verifiable Domains

Rubric-based methods address this by decomposing evaluation into explicit criteria, but existing approaches typically depend on frontier LLMs and suffe
articles.summary: 提出RUBRIC-ARROW，交替点状评分奖励建模方法，通过显式标准分解评估，解决非可验证领域中的主观评分问题。

articles.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
articles.excerpt: Robot manipulation critically depends on perception that preserves the action-relevant aspects of a scene. Yet most robot learning pipelines are built upon visual encoders pre-trained for static recognition or vision-language alignment, leaving motion understanding to downstream policies. We introdu
articles.summary: 提出DynaFLIP，三模态（视觉、触觉、动力学）引导的表示学习，用于机器人操作感知，增强运动理解。

articles.title: REPOT: Recoverable Program-of-Thought via Checkpoint Repair
articles.excerpt: One-shot Program-of-Thought (PoT) emits a Python program that prints a primitive-action plan; a single invalid action silently invalidates the trajectory. We introduce RePoT (Recoverable PoT): a deterministic verified replay that walks the plan through the environment to its first invalid transition
